name: Brain
description: The control center of the body. Many food additives can cross the blood-brain barrier and affect neurotransmitter function, leading to headaches, cognitive decline, and mood disorders.

name: Liver
description: The body's primary detoxification organ. Processes chemicals, preservatives, and additives. Overexposure can overwhelm liver function and lead to fatty liver disease and toxicity.

name: Kidneys
description: Filter waste products and toxins from the blood. Artificial additives and high sodium levels put significant strain on kidney function over time.

name: Stomach
description: The first major digestive organ where food breakdown begins. Harsh additives and preservatives can irritate the stomach lining and disrupt the gut microbiome.

name: Heart
description: Pumps blood throughout the body. High sodium, caffeine, and certain preservatives can elevate blood pressure and strain cardiovascular function.

name: Lungs
description: Responsible for oxygen exchange. Certain food chemicals and preservatives can trigger inflammatory responses affecting respiratory health.
